chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.2170 of 2025 (Tehseen vs. The State) Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 11.12.2025 Mr. Allah Ditta Shakir, advocate for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Salim, Addl: PG Sindh O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.28/2025, u/s 6/9(C) of CNS Amendment Act, 2022, registered at Police Station Boat Basin, Karachi by means of this application. 2. Applicant and co-accused were arrested by a police team of Police Station Boat Basin on 17.01.2025 from Block-1, near Sea Rock Apartment Clifton, Karachi, and from them respectively 1290 and 1050 grams of charas were recovered, hence, they were booked in the present case. 3. Bail application of the applicant was dismissed by the trial Court by impugned order dated 28.02.2025. However, subsequently, the same Court has granted bail to co-accused Muhammad Ibrahim, from whom 1050 grams of charas was recovered. 4. Learned counsel for the applicant has prayed for grant bail on rule of consistency. 5. Learned Addl: PG Sindh has opposed bail but we are of the view that the rule of consistency is applicable in the present case as co-accused Muhammad Ibrahim, from whom almost same quantity of charas was recovered has been granted bail. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/ (Rupees fifty thousand only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 6.

Learned Addl: PG Sindh has opposed bail but we are of the view that the rule of consistency is applicable in the present case as co-accused Muhammad Ibrahim, from whom almost same quantity of charas was recovered has been granted bail. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/ (Rupees fifty thousand only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 6. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
